attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place a box with a plate on a table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene contains a dining area with a white rectangular table and several wooden chairs. The floor is made of light-colored wood planks. The walls are white, and there is a dark-framed window or cabinet visible in the background.

Relevant Objects and Their Types:

1. Table (receptacle): A large white rectangular table with four legs. It is a receptacle as it can hold objects. Currently, it holds:
   - A golden statuette on a circular base (object, not a receptacle).
   - A folded newspaper (object, not a receptacle).
   - A plate with a decorative rim (object, not a receptacle) — visible in a different view of the same table.
   - A small set of keys (object, not a receptacle) — visible in another view.

2. Box (object): A brown cardboard box, open and empty, resting on a white countertop or shelf near a window. It is not a receptacle in the domain definition, but visually it can hold items. It is not currently on the table.

3. Microwave (receptacle): A white microwave with a black front panel, located on a countertop. It is openable and currently closed. It is not interacting with any object.

4. Sink (receptacle): Not visible in any of the provided images.

5. Fridge (receptacle): Not visible in any of the provided images.

6. Knife (object): Not visible in any of the provided images.

Spatial Relationships and Current State:

- The robot is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).
- The brown box is on a white countertop/shelf, not on the table. It is open and empty.
- The plate is on the white table. It is not inside any receptacle.
- The golden statuette and newspaper are also on the table.
- The microwave is on a countertop and is closed.
- The table is surrounded by at least two wooden chairs.
- No object is currently being held by the agent.
- No object is marked as clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled.
- The microwave is not openable in the current view, but it is defined as openable in the domain.
- The box is not a receptacle in the domain, so it cannot be used to hold objects in the planning logic, despite its physical capability.

Colors and Visual Properties:

- The table is white with a smooth surface.
- The chairs are dark brown wood.
- The box is brown cardboard, with flaps open.
- The plate is white with a blue and red decorative rim.
- The statuette is golden and metallic.
- The newspaper is grayish with visible text.
- The microwave is white with a black front panel and a blue circular detail on the left side.
- The countertop/shelf is white and flat.
- The floor is light wood with visible grain.

The task requires placing a box with a plate on the table. Currently, the box is not on the table, and the plate is already on the table but not inside the box. The box must be moved to the table and the plate placed inside it, but since the box is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, this may require redefining the box as a receptacle or using an alternative approach.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_box_with_plate_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        table - receptacle
        box - object
        plate - object
        microwave - receptacle
        statuette - object
        newspaper - object
        keys - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 table))
        (not (atLocation agent1 box))
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave))
        (not (atLocation agent1 plate))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (opened microwave))
        (openable microwave)
        (inReceptacle plate table)
        (inReceptacle statuette table)
        (inReceptacle newspaper table)
        (inReceptacle keys table)
        (not (isClean plate))
        (not (isHot plate))
        (not (isCool plate))
        (not (isSliced plate))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle plate box)
            (inReceptacle box table)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_box_with_plate_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        table - receptacle
        box - object
        plate - object
        microwave - receptacle
        statuette - object
        newspaper - object
        keys - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 table))
        (not (atLocation agent1 box))
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave))
        (not (atLocation agent1 plate))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (opened microwave))
        (openable microwave)
        (inReceptacle plate table)
        (inReceptacle statuette table)
        (inReceptacle newspaper table)
        (inReceptacle keys table)
        (not (isClean plate))
        (not (isHot plate))
        (not (isCool plate))
        (not (isSliced plate))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle plate box)
            (inReceptacle box table)
        )
    )
)

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains the following objects relevant to the domain and instruction:

1. **Objects and their types:**
   - **Receptacles:**
     - A white rectangular table (receptacle) located in the center of the room, with a light wood floor beneath it. It is not openable.
     - A white countertop or shelf (receptacle) against the back wall, adjacent to a window. It is not openable.
     - A microwave (receptacle) is partially visible on the left side of the countertop; it is openable.
     - A fridge (receptacle) is not visible in any image.
     - A sink (receptacle) is not visible in any image.
   - **Objects:**
     - A brown cardboard box (object) with flaps open, resting on the white countertop/shelf. It is not a receptacle.
     - A round plate (object) with a decorative rim, located on the white table. It is not a receptacle.
     - A golden statuette (object) on the table, not relevant to the domain.
     - A rolled-up newspaper (object) on the table, not relevant to the domain.
     - A set of keys (object) on the table, not relevant to the domain.
     - A knife (object) is not visible in any image.

2. **Quantities:**
   - 1 microwave (receptacle)
   - 1 white table (receptacle)
   - 1 white countertop/shelf (receptacle)
   - 1 brown cardboard box (object)
   - 1 plate (object)

3. **Spatial relationships:**
   - The brown cardboard box is on the white countertop/shelf.
   - The plate is on the white table.
   - The table is surrounded by four dark wooden chairs.
   - The countertop/shelf is against the back wall, to the left of a window.
   - The microwave is on the countertop/shelf, to the left of the cardboard box.

4. **Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:**
   - The microwave is closed (not opened).
   - The cardboard box is not held by any agent and is not in any receptacle (it is on the countertop/shelf, which is a receptacle, so it is “in” that receptacle).
   - The plate is not held by any agent and is on the table (a receptacle), so it is “in” that receptacle.
   - No object is currently held by the agent (the agent is not present in the scene).
   - No object is clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled.
   - The agent is not at any location (initial state).

5. **Colors and visual properties:**
   - The cardboard box is medium brown with visible creases and open flaps.
   - The plate is white with a purple and blue decorative rim.
   - The table is white with curved legs.
   - The countertop/shelf is white and flat.
   - The microwave is white with a black front panel.
   - The floor is light wood with visible grain.
   - The window has a dark gray frame.

The task requires placing the box with the plate on the table. Currently, the box is on the countertop/shelf and the plate is on the table. The robot must pick up the box, then pick up the plate (or vice versa), and place them together on the table. Since the plate is already on the table, the robot may only need to move the box to the table and place it near or under the plate, depending on interpretation. However, the instruction implies the plate should be placed “on” the box, which is not currently the case. Therefore, the robot must move the plate onto the box after placing the box on the table, or place the box on the table and then place the plate on top of it.

(define (problem place_box_with_plate)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        microwave1 - microwave
        table1 - receptacle
        countertop1 - receptacle
        box1 - object
        plate1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (inReceptacle box1 countertop1)
        (inReceptacle plate1 table1)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean plate1))
        (not (isHot plate1))
        (not (isCool plate1))
        (not (isSliced plate1))
        (not (isOn microwave1))
        (not (isToggled microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 table1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 countertop1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 box1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 plate1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle box1 table1)
        (inReceptacle plate1 box1)
    ))
)

 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
